name: Racing Game
description: A 3D racing game with car controller, track with checkpoints, lap timing, speed boost, and opponent AI. Targets Three.js with custom car physics or Rapier raycast vehicle.

requiredElements.name: car-controller
requiredElements.description: Car with acceleration, steering, drift, and friction model.

requiredElements.name: track-checkpoints
requiredElements.description: Track with checkpoint sequence, lap counting, and wrong-way detection.

requiredElements.name: lap-timer
requiredElements.description: Lap time display with best lap, current lap, and delta to best.

requiredElements.name: camera-follow
requiredElements.description: Chase camera that follows the car with position lerp and look-ahead.

requiredElements.name: speed-feedback
requiredElements.description: Speed lines/FOV widen/sound pitch at high speed for sense of velocity.

optionalElements.name: opponent-ai
optionalElements.description: AI cars that follow racing line with rubber-banding.

optionalElements.name: drift-mechanic
optionalElements.description: Handbrake drift with tire smoke particles and lateral friction reduction.

optionalElements.name: boost-system
optionalElements.description: Nitro boost with limited charges and visual flame trail.

optionalElements.name: minimap
optionalElements.description: Top-down minimap showing track, player, and opponents.

qualityRules.description: FOV widens at speed, camera pulls back, speed lines appear — velocity is felt.
Drift has tire smoke, sound, and controlled slide feel.
Chase camera is smooth, never clips through walls, lerp-damped.
Car physics model is stable at all speeds; no flipping or flying off track.
Checkpoint/lap logic is robust — no skipping, no false triggers.
Track geometry is optimized; instanced barriers; no per-frame alloc in car update.
Track environment has scenery, lighting, and atmosphere; road texture reads at speed.
Speedometer, lap timer, position — all readable at a glance during fast play.

antiPatterns.description: Car moves but there's no sense of speed — feels like driving at 20mph.
Chase camera clips through walls or ground — jarring visual break.
Car flips over on small bumps and cannot recover — frustrating.
Player can skip checkpoints by cutting corners — breaks lap timing.

improvementSuggestions.description: Widen camera FOV from 60 to 80 as speed increases for velocity feel.
Add tire smoke particles during drift and hard braking.
Add roadside scenery (trees, barriers, grandstands) for speed reference.

exampleOutputs.label: Chase camera with lerp

references.title: GDC — The Physics of Racing Games
references.author: Various
